help.concept: `cp` leaves the original file in place and creates a copy.
help.hint_2: Run the command in the terminal, check the response, then submit.

help.concept: `mv` renames a file when the source and destination are in the same folder.

cp-copies.title: Copy keeps the original
cp-copies.bodyMarkdown:
`cp` makes another copy of a file. Use it when you want a backup or a second version.

```bash
cp inbox/report.txt archive/report-copy.txt
```

mv-moves-renames.title: `mv` moves or renames
mv-moves-renames.bodyMarkdown: `mv` can move a file into another folder or rename it in the same folder. The destination path decides what happens.

rm-safe-habits.title: Remove only the file you mean
rm-safe-habits.bodyMarkdown: `rm` deletes files. Before using it, run `ls` and check the exact filename. In this course, removal stays inside the lesson workspace.
